It outlines the road's beginning at the Lexington city limits and its end at the Scott County line, a distance of 7.9 miles. The construction, maintenance, supervision, and control of this road project are to be governed by the terms set forth in chapter 17 of the Acts of the General Assembly of 1920.
classification.reasoning: The statute describes the designation of a specific road as part of the state's primary road system. It details the road's location and references previous legislation governing state roads. There is no mention of race, nor are there any provisions that implicitly or explicitly discriminate based on race. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law.

classification.summary: This act establishes a new road as part of Kentucky's primary system of state roads and highways. The road will connect Federal Highway No. 60 east of Owensboro, Daviess County, passing through Thurston, Ensor, Knottsville (Daviess County), Pellville, and Patesville (Hancock County), and will terminate at Federal Highway No. 60 near Cloverport in Breckinridge County. The construction, maintenance, supervision, and control of this road will follow the same terms and conditions as established for the primary system of state roads in the Acts of the General Assembly, 1920, Chapter 17, page 76.

It details the route and references previous legislation for its construction and maintenance. There is no mention of race, nor any provisions that would implicitly or explicitly discriminate based on race. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law.

Commonwealth of Kentucky :

Barnett's creek road , same connecting the Columbia-
Dunnville road , State Project No. 23 , with the Camp-
bellsville -Liberty road , Project No. 154 , and that

CHAPTER 455 .
AN ACT repealing Chapter 181 of the Acts of the General Assem-

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. There is hereby established as a part of the
primary system of state highways of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky a road from a point on the Bos-
worth Trail at or near Big Hill Post Office , in
Madison County, thence via Berea and within one
mile of Wallacetown to a point on the state highway
at or near Paint Lick , in Garrard County.

and control of said road shall be subject to and con-
trolled by the provisions of Chapter 17 of the Acts
of the General Assembly as amended , to the same
effect as if said road had been named in the original
act as one of the projects of the primary system of
state highways as provided therein .
§ 3. Chapter 181 of the Acts of the General
Assembly of 1928 is hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

CHAPTER 456 .
AN ACT establishing as a part of primary system of state high-
ways , a road from Somerset , Pulaski county , to Mt. Vernon ,
Rockcastle County .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That there be established as a part of the
primary road system of the state highways of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , a road beginning at
the most practical point on what is known as the
Swiss Colony Route of Somerset and London road
as located and approved by the State Highway
Commission on December 11th , 1928 , and running
to Mt. Vernon , Rockcastle County , over the most
practical route . Said point of beginning and route
to be determined by the Highway Commission. The
purpose of this act is to enable the Highway Com-
mission to use a portion of said Swiss Colony Route
of Somerset and London road as a double interseat
route , from Somerset , Pulaski County, to London ,
Laurel County , and from Somerset , Pulaski County ,
to Mt. Vernon , Rockcastle County .
§ 2. The construction, maintenance , supervision
and control of said road shall be subject to all the
terms and conditions set out and provided for in
Chapter 17 of the Acts of the General Assembly of
1920 establishing and creating a primary system of
state highways .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

CHAPTER 521 .
JOINT RESOLUTION providing for the appointment of a com-
mittee to investigate the conduct and affairs of the State High-
way Commission ; members of the State Highway Commission ,
officers , employees and agents thereof ; clothing said commit-
tee with plenary power to subpoena witnesses , require the
production of papers , documents , contracts , proposed con-
tracts ; making an appropriation of $ 20,000 for the expenses
of said committee ; setting out the manner of organization of
said committee ; fixing the time for which preliminary report
shall be made to the General Assembly ; directing the commit-
tee to employ all assistants deemed expedient ; calling upon
the Attorney General and all other state officials and em-
ployes to respond to requests made by said committee , and
directing the Sergeant -at -Arms of the House of Representa-
tives and Senate , and the sheriffs of the various counties to

WHEREAS , The State Highway Commission has been brought

Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the

CHAPTER 523 .
RESOLUTION authorizing Charles W. Caldwell of Danville to
sue the Commonwealth of Kentucky .
WHEREAS , in March , 1926 , Charles W. Caldwell was the owner
and holder of three road warrants totalling $24,768.23 and
bearing five per cent interest payable semi -annually , and ,
WHEREAS , said warrants were called on March 26th , 1926 , and
were paid about August 1st , 1926 , and said payment was for
the amount of said warrants together with interest due until
said warrants were called , and ,
WHEREAS , Charles W. Caldwell is claiming the sum of $399.06
as interest due on these warrants from the date they were
called until the date they were paid , now therefore ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Charles W. Caldwell be , and he is hereby,
authorized and permitted to sue the Commonwealth
of Kentucky in the Franklin Circuit Court for such
interest as may be due him if any is , and in the event
a judgment is recovered by said Charles W. Caldwell
in said suit it shall be paid by the Auditor of the
State of Kentucky by warrant on the Treasurer
without execution , but the judgment shall not exceed
the sum of $399.06 , and the Commonwealth shall
have the right to appeal as in any other civil case .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

CHAPTER 530 .
RESOLUTION inviting a representative of the Cumberland
River Hydro -Electric Company to present its views and explain
its proposition in connection with the erection of a power dam
and the creation of a state park.
WHEREAS , an invitation has been extended to Senator Coleman
DuPont , of Delaware , to appear before a joint session of the
Legislature for the purpose of explaining his reputed propo-
sition to purchase the Cumberland Falls and adjacent terri-
tory, and to donate same to the State Park Commission of
Kentucky for state park purposes , and
WHEREAS , it is represented that the Cumberland River Hydro-
Electric Company is proposing to erect a power dam on the
Cumberland River , in the vicinity of said Cumberland Falls ,
at an expense of approximately seven million dollars , in such
a way that it claims that the said Falls will not be molested
nor the scenic beauty of same , or surroundings , impaired , and
WHEREAS , it is further represented that the citizens in the
section of the State which is involved are practically a unit
in favor of the combined power project and state park, and
WHEREAS , Senator DuPont and the citizens of southeastern
Kentucky have been extended an invitation to appear before
a joint session of the Legislature for the purpose of presenting
their views on this subject ;
Now , therefore , be it resolved :
That the Cumberland River Hydro -Electric Com-
pany also be invited to appear before this joint ses-
sion and present its views and explain its proposi-
tion in connection with the erection of said power
dam and the creation of a state park .
Approved February 5, 1930 .

CHAPTER 531 .
JOINT RESOLUTION of the Senate and House of Repre-

Be it resolved by the Senate and House of Repre-
sentatives of the Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That the Lieutenant Governor be requested to
immediately appoint a committee of five from the

membership of the House of Representatives to com-
pose a joint committee for the purpose of consider-
ing proposed and needed legislation concerning toll
bridges and the issuance of revenue bonds therefor .
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that said joint commit-
tee shall meet at one-thirty o'clock P. M. , Thursday ,
February 6th , and organize and proceed to give con-
sideration to said proposed and needed legislation ,
and to make a report of their recommendations to
the Senate and House of Representatives as early
as possible .
Approved February 12 , 1930 .
CHAPTER 532 .
A RESOLUTION inviting Ex- Senator Luke Lee of Tennessee to
address the General Assembly on the tax laws of his State .
WHEREAS , the State of Tennessee has taken an advanced
position on the question of the taxation of farm lands , having
exempted entirely all such lands from taxation , and
WHEREAS , Ex-United States Senator Luke Lee of said State
is an authority on taxation and has taken a prominent part
in the passage of the present taxation laws of that State , and
WHEREAS , this body would like to be enlightened upon the new
taxation laws of that State , and
WHEREAS , the new revenue laws of said State are proving
very satisfactory to the people of our sister Commonwealth
to the south and providing the necessary amount of money
for all the needs of that State .

because of their proximity to the State of Ohio and the saving
effected in transportation, which is not consistent in view of
the fact that the extreme northern and eastern counties of the
State could more economically be sent to the Charleston
(West Virginia ) Regional office and if this policy was to be
consistently followed by the Veterans ' Bureau in the assign-
ment of territory to the Regional offices it would be more
economical to assign southern Illinois to the St. Louis ( Mis-
souri ) Regional office than to send men from this territory
to the Chicago Regional office as is now being done , which
would seem to indicate that the question of cost of transpor-
tation is not the controlling factor , and
WHEREAS , the assignment of territory to the Regional offices

CHAPTER 537 .
JOINT RESOLUTION of the General Assembly of Kentucky .
WHEREAS , there are now pending before Congress two bills
designated as House Resolution 8000 and Senate Bill 3073 , to
provide for the designation and construction of national park
approach roads , and
WHEREAS , said bills provide that whenever the Secretary of
Interior shall determine it to be in the public interest he may
designate as National Park approach roads and as supple-
mentary parts of the Highway System any of the National
Park roads whose primary value is to carry National Park
travel and which lead across lands wholly or partly owned by
the Federal Government , and which will connect the
highways within a national park with a convenient point on
the Federal Aid Highway System . Such approach roads so
designated , under the Bill , would be limited not to exceed sixty
miles in length between a park gateway and the nearest con-
venient Federal Aid road , or where such an approach road is
carried on the Federal Aid System , it shall be limited not to
exceed thirty miles . For the purpose of carrying out this pro-
gram , the Secretary of the Interior would be authorized to
reconstruct and improve such national park approach roads
so designated and the sum of five million dollars would be
authorized to be appropriated for the fiscal year ending June
30 , 1931 , the sum of five million dollars for the fiscal year

monwealth of Kentucky memorializing the President of the

WHEREAS , the Honorable Simeon S. Willis , of the Court of
Appeals of Kentucky , has distinguished himself by the clear'

to his labors at the bar and on the bench , and
WHEREAS , it is deemed fitting that the successor of the late
Mr. Justice Sanford should come from the South ,
BE IT THEREFORE RESOLVED by the General Assem-

President of the United States be memorialized to
appoint Judge Willis to the Supreme Court .
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED , That the Chief Clerk of
the Senate forward a copy of this resoultion to the
President of the United States .

CHAPTER 543 .
RESOLUTION for the benefit of A. J. Bush, of Elizabethtown ,
Hardin County , Kentucky .
WHEREAS , That A. J. Bush be and he is hereby allowed and
authorized and permitted to sue the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky in the Circuit Court of his residence for damages not
exceeding five thousand dollars , as he may have suffered , if
any , by reason of a personal injury so suffered by said A. J.
Bush on the second day of February , 1924. In the event dam-
ages are recovered by the said Bush for said injury same to
be paid by the Auditor by warrant on the treasury .
WHEREAS , his hip bone was fractured and driven through his
hip joint , and he was otherwise seriously, painfully , and per-
manently injured , disabling and incapacitating him from
manual labor for the remainder of his life , and
WHEREAS , said A. J. Bush is now 64 years of age and was at
the time of his said injury earning $4.00 per day , and was
because of said injury required to expend the sum of $ 1200.00
for medical attention , hospital bills , & c., and is now unable
to do manual labor, and has no education , being unable to
read or write , it is now the expressed sense of the General
Assembly that said A. J. Bush should be compensated for said
injuries and resultant loss of earning power .
Be it therefore resolved by the General Assembly
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky :

CHAPTER 544 .
RESOLUTION relating to the Control of Motor Vehicles for Hire
Engaged in Interstate Commerce .
WHEREAS , in the absence of control , there has grown up a
class of interstate motor bus operation which is entirely
unregulated and altogether irresponsible and subject to the
regulations of no state or federal authority whatever , and
WHEREAS , the traveling public is being constantly victimized
by such operators crossing state lines between Kentucky and
neighboring states , and who resort to all sorts of illegitimate
and unscrupulous activities at the expense of travelers who
are unable to obtain redress , and
WHEREAS , the regularly certified bus lines operating under
Commission of Motor Transportation are a source of large
revenue to the state , and now constitute one of the state's
major public utilities , and
WHEREAS , the activities of the unregulated interstate "wild-
cat " operators subject both the legitimate bus lines and other
forms of transportation to unfair and injurious and unscrupu-
lous competition , and
WHEREAS , the present condition is highly detrimental to the
interests of the State of Kentucky , to the legitimate trans-
portation interests , to the traveling public and to the public
generally , and
WHEREAS , such conditions present an urgent need for adequate
Federal regulation , at least as to proper certification and con-
trol , now , therefore,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
•

CHAPTER 546 .
RESOLUTION of the benefit of Charles Brown of Whitley
County , Kentucky .
WHEREAS , on the twenty-eighth day of January , 1923 , Charles
Brown , who was then employed by the State Highway Com-
mission of the Commonwealth of Kentucky as rodman , whilst
in the performance of his duties as such rodman was seriously ,
painfully and permanently injured by the fact that another
employee named Allie Willson of the State Highway Com-
mission pitched a grade stake to the said Charles Brown which
struck him in the left eye and put the eye practically out ,
incapacitating him from manual labor and necessitating the
expenditure of a considerable sum of money for medical
attention , hospital bills , etc.
WHEREAS , said Charles Brown is now thirty -three years of age
and was at the time of his said injury earning one hundred
dollars per month and was because of said injury required to
expend the sum of eight hundred dollars for medical attention ,
hospital bills , etc. , and is yet under the care of a physician
and unable to do manual labor , it is now the expressed sense
of the General Assembly that said Charles Brown should be
compensated for said injury and resultant loss of earning
power .
THEREFORE , the said Charles Brown is hereby
granted the privilege to sue the Commonwealth of
Kentucky for such damages he may have sustained .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

930 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
BE IT RESOLVED by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
WHEREAS , under Sections 1999a -1 to 1999a -8 "every person ,
corporation or association employing any person to act as
agent or counsel to promote or oppose in any manner the pas-
sage by the legislature of any legislation affecting the pecu-
niary interests of any individual , association or corporation
as distinct from those of the whole people of the state ," and
WHEREAS , said persons are required under the law to register
in the office of the Attorney General , giving their names and
addresses , and the company that they represent , and
WHEREAS , there are many such persons now engaged in lobby-
ing in favor of and against important legislation now pending
before the General Assembly of Kentucky , and
WHEREAS , the members composing the Senate and House of
Representatives come from all sections of the State and are
unacquainted with such lobbyists and are unable to judge of
the sincerity of their motives and purposes without knowing
who they are and what they represent , and
WHEREAS , it is not convenient for the different members of
the General Asesmbly to visit the Attorney General's office
and to examine said register from time to time without great
inconvenience and loss of time,
Now , THEREFORE , Be it resolved that the Attorney
General of this State be requested to furnish a list
on Tuesday, February 4, 1930 , and on each Monday
thereafter of all registered lobbyists , giving their
names , residence and the company that they repre-
sent , and that he be requested to furnish on each
Monday thereafter , as long as this legislature is in
session , such additional lobbyists with their names ,
addresses and company that they represent to the
House and Senate so that they may be informed as
to who are lobbyists and what company they repre-
sent . That the clerk be requested to have printed
and placed on the desk of each member of the House
and Senate a list of all said lobbyists so furnished ,
giving their names , addresses and the companies
that they represent .
Approved February 5, 1930 .

WHEREAS , the three State Asylums for the Insane and the
State Feeble-Minded Institute , and the two State Reformator-
ies and the State's Tuberculosis Sanatorium , all are said to be
crowded far beyond capacity , causing very bad sanitary condi-
tions to exist in all of the State Institutions , and making treat-
ment of disease extremely difficult and the danger of any
epidemic or from fire alarming , and
WHEREAS , it would be criminal negligence on the part of this
Legislature to adjourn without investigating these reported
conditions and providing some relief therefor ;
BE IT RESOLVED by the House of Representatives
and the Senate of the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,
that the Speaker of the House be and he is hereby
empowered to and shall appoint a committee of three
from the personnel of the House , and the Lieutenant
Governor , a committee of three from the personnel
of the Senate , said committees to act jointly and as
one committee to investigate and ascertain as near
as possible the true conditions existing in these said
institutions and to report same together with recom-

CHAPTER 553 .
RESOLUTION authorizing Claude Henderson by his statutory
guardian or next friend to sue the Commonwealth of Kentucky
and the State Highway Commission , or either.
WHEREAS , on March 4th , 1929 , the State Highway Commission
was engaged in road construction in certain counties in Eastern
Kentucky , and in the course of said road construction it main-
tained a garage , warehouse and storeroom on Greenup Avenue ,
in the City of Ashland , Kentucky , and ,
WHEREAS , it kept , used and maintained quantities of dynamite
which was exploded by dynamite caps , and ,
WHEREAS , it is alleged that it negligently placed or left on the
outside of said garage building and near the street in the city
of Ashland , a large number of said dynamite caps where they
were easily accessible and attractive to children , and ,
WHEREAS , the said Claude Henderson , an infant fourteen years
of age , in company with other small children , came in contact
with said dynamite caps which had been so left upon or near
said street and through curiosity was handling some of said
dynamite caps , not knowing what they were or that they were
dangerous , and without such knowledge one of said dynamite
caps exploded with such force and violence that it severed one
of his hands from his arm and he has lost said hand and a
portion of his arm , and ,
WHEREAS , his parents are in humble circumstances and unable
to properly educate said child , and ,
WHEREAS , he has sustained this serious injury and lost his
hand through the alleged carelessness of the employees of the
State Highway Commission in the conduct of the State's busi-
ness , therefore,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Claude Henderson an infant of Ashland ,
Kentucky , be and he is hereby authorized , empow-
ered and permitted by his statutory guardian or

WHEREAS , at the close of the fiscal year , June 30th , 1929 ,
when the Board had only functioned since March 21st , 1929 ,
the said Board turned back to the Treasurer of the Common-

and sixty-three one -hundredths ( $1,773.63 ) dollars , unex-
